videos.title: 12 ESSENTIAL Travel Tips when Visiting Petra & Jordan | Full Travel Guide
videos.description:
We cover literally everything about visiting Petra and Jordan: How long to stay, how to get the best views, The Jordan Pass, visa entry, public transport, the cost of everything, which hotels to stay in, tips for visiting the Dead Sea, renting a car, tour guides, safety and more in 2023. Below are all the links mentioned.  

👉 *_TOUR PACKAGES and HOTEL LINKS BELOW_* 

*_TOURS AROUND JORDAN_*

👉* 6 Day tour of Jordan, similar to ours: https://www.viator.com/tours/Amman/6-Day-Tour-Amman-Petra-Jerash-Baptism-Site-Karak-Castle-Wadi-Rum-Red-and-Dead-Sea/d5503-18249P27?pid=P00054239&mcid=42383&medium=link&campaign=JordanTips 

👉* Alternate 6 Day Jordan tour: https://www.viator.com/tours/Amman/6-Night-Best-of-Jordan-Tour-from-Amman-Jerash-Dead-Sea-Petra-and-Wadi-Rum/d5503-2268ZTT01GD5N?pid=P00054239&mcid=42383&medium=link&campaign=JordanTips 

👉 Tour that we took, as seen in video: https://www.intrepidtravel.com/uk/jordan/premium-jordan-134598#Js-QTZhxqyZCbAaMWBGnwDNmljtbqVPtig_gWF0Qsow6

👉* 3 Day Tour of Petra, Wadi Rum and Dead Sea: https://www.getyourguide.com/amman-l1035/from-amman-petra-rum-aqaba-dead-sea-3-day-tour-t62162/?partner_id=WMA142S&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-top&cmp=JordanTips

👉* Day trip to The Dead Sea from Amman: https://www.getyourguide.com/amman-l1035/dead-sea-and-madaba-tour-from-amman-t62138/?partner_id=WMA142S&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-middle&cmp=JordanTips

👉* Private tour of Jerash: https://www.getyourguide.com/umm-qais-l1648/from-amman-jerash-ajloun-castle-umm-qais-private-tour-t369141/?partner_id=WMA142S&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-top&cmp=JordanTips

👉* Petra Private Day Trip: https://www.getyourguide.com/amman-l1035/from-amman-petra-day-tour-t93864/?partner_id=WMA142S&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-top&cmp=JordanTips

👉 Watch the full video of the ALTERNATE PETRA route to the view of the Treasury from Travelling Tom: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XmQ_SUEndcY&t=596s

*_HOTELS we stayed at (and then alternatives)_*

👉* Amman rated as "Fabulous": https://www.booking.com/hotel/jo/sulaf-luxury.en.html?aid=1864334&no_rooms=1&group_adults=2&label=JordanTips

👉* Amman alternative hotel rated as "Superb", closer to the centre which I'd suggest is better: https://www.booking.com/hotel/jo/nu-fifty-two.en.html?aid=1864334&no_rooms=1&group_adults=2&label=JordanTips

👉* 5 Star Dead Sea rated as "Very Good": https://www.booking.com/hotel/jo/holiday-inn-resort-dead-sea.en.html?aid=1864334&no_rooms=1&group_adults=2&label=JordanTips

👉* Dead Sea alternative rated as "Very Good", Have to take taxi to Dead Sea but looks amazing: https://www.booking.com/hotel/jo/evason-ma-in-hot-springs-six-senses-spa.en.html?aid=1864334&no_rooms=1&group_adults=2&label=JordanTips

👉* Petra rated as "Superb": https://www.booking.com/hotel/jo/petra-moon.en.html?aid=1864334&no_rooms=1&group_adults=2&label=JordanTips

👉* Petra alternative rated "very good": https://www.booking.com/hotel/jo/moevenpick-resort-petra.en.html?aid=1864334&no_rooms=1&group_adults=2&label=JordanTips

👉* Wadi Rum rated as "Very Good" https://www.booking.com/hotel/jo/wadi-rum-nights-camp.en.html?aid=1864334&no_rooms=1&group_adults=2&label=JordanTips

👉* Wadi Rum alternative rated as "superb": https://www.booking.com/hotel/jo/rum-magic-nights.en.html?aid=1864334&no_rooms=1&group_adults=2&label=JordanTips

Travelled in April.

videos.transcript: [Music] don't think the country is cheap now yeah you can get good food that's cheap if you just want to go for falafel and hummus but it can get quite expensive especially the accommodation now for a hostel you pay around 15 to 20 per night and if you want to have a hotel room it very easily goes to 50 60 70 100 per night so don't make the mistake of just thinking it's a very cheap country bear that in mind and just look for good deals online before you go there don't forget to book the tours before you go to the buddy rum desert now if you want to do a tour through the vadiram you know you're sleeping in one of those camps you want to do a tour book one of those tours in the experiences beforehand because if you arrive at the camp and you know you want to haggle it won't work do the before see where they have the best prices maybe you know get together with some people do together that will pull down the price don't just shake hands with the women there wait if they offer you the hand it's a muslim country so a lot of the women there don't shake hands with men so you know be respectful and if the woman offers you to shake the hand shake the hand otherwise don't just you know point your your hand to the woman because you know maybe they don't want to do that don't forget to haggle now it's an arab country where a lot of people haggle it's very normal especially when you go to the markets so don't forget to do that because the price that you hear in the beginning is not the price that in the end you have to pay or that people pay it's normal you know to haggle and and talk about the price now one tip i can give you is to speak to locals and ask them maybe your guide or the hotel you know like i want to buy the scarf at the market how much should i pay what's normal because then you have an idea of you know going to the market of how much it should cost and you know how much you can haggle don't try diving in the dead sea now it's very salty and diving is not possible because of the buoyancy it's 34 salt which just will make your eyes burn when you try to dive into it how do i know i tried it not a good idea so don't do that another thing is to be careful you know when you shave before because um all these little wounds you know when you go in the dead sea the salt will come in and it will burn like crazy don't shave before don't try to dive otherwise just enjoy floating in the dead sea don't think jordan is just desert and hot now it is desert and it can get hot but also it can get quite cold especially in the desert at night so bring something warm and there's other places there's even areas where it snows so just keep that in mind there's a lot of variety you know the red sea with beautiful beaches the desert of course some mountains lush and nice canyons so much to see a lot of variety not just desert and super hot don't forget to buy the jordan pass before you visit the country now now this pass covers the visa fee the entrance to petra and the entrance to a lot of other sites a lot of people think you can get the jordan pass at the airport but that's wrong you have to get it before so don't make that mistake don't fall for the scams especially at petra there's a lot of tourists coming and when there's a lot of tourists you know people sometimes try to scan them let's just say it how it is unfortunately and one of those scams is the horseback rides now it will say in your ticket that the horseback rides are included and they technically are but the people will expect a hefty tip so i recommend you not to do it plus it's just a short walk anyways don't do it because you will start arguing with the people there if you say it's free and it's just a very unpleasant experience furthermore you know there's a lot of things you can buy in in petra and sometimes they will put a wristband on your arm and say it's free but then you know they want you to give them money just try to be aware and especially in the touristy places such as petra avoid the scams and don't fall for them if you're going to jordan don't feel uncomfortable if the guy grabs your hand to just show you around now this is especially for guys men there you know they hold hands while they walk around especially when they get along well now it's nothing to do with being gay or whatsoever it's just the way it's done there may be a bit uncomfortable for us but you know if you really want to take a deep dive into the culture just go along with it happened to me several times the guy just took the hand it feels a bit uncomfortable but it's just the way it's done there now last but not least don't forget to say shukran this is maybe one of the most important things at least that's what i think shukran means thank you and it's always nice you know people always appreciate when you just learn a little bit of their language and i mean to say thank you that's the least you can do shukran thank you very easy to remember people will be happy if you know a bit and if you keep those things in mind i guarantee you you will have a fantastic fantastic time in jordan it's a beautiful country so just don't do these 10 things and you will have a fine and fantastic time

safetyNotes: BUT be careful when you travel to Jordan, because there is a few things that you should not do

videos.title: Petra Travel Guide:  YOU MUST Know These Tips
videos.description:
We are sharing the 8 things we think you need to know when planning your trip to Petra! We traveled to this wonder of the world in December 2021 - January 2022 and are sharing our experience in hopes that it will simplify your travel planning. In this video we cover everything from the logistics of the the Jordan Pass (and your entry visa), renting a car, and which hotels we stayed at all the way to what we suggest you do with your time inside the UNESCO World Heritage site. 

Looking for specific tips? Check out these chapters! 
00:00 Intro
00:20 Tip 1: The Jordan Pass
00:59 Tip 2: Renting a car
01:24 Tip 3: Staying in Wadi Musa
02:07 Tip 4: The right footware
02:35 Tip 5: Where to hike
03:55 Tip 6: Tours in Petra
04:20 Tip 7: Shopping in Petra
4:50 Tip 8: Petra by Night

Check out our travel vlog of our time at Petra from 7am to 11pm and see what we got up to during our time in Wadi Musa:
https://youtu.be/bkPJtYMR1pQ

Disclaimer:  Some of the links in this video description are affiliate links, which means that if you click on one of the product links and make a purchase, I may receive a small commission. This helps support the channel and allows me to continue making videos like this. Thank you for your support!

Rode Wireless Microphones:  https://amzn.to/428oWcq
Camera Tripod:  https://amzn.to/41biu34

Our Current Favorite Travel Product:    https://youtube.com/shorts/GAYXNwV3ZrI?feature=share
videos.transcript: after walking 17 miles from sun up to sun down we're ready to share everything we think you'll want to know so you can plan your dream trip to Petra ready to go in hopes that you too will feel like this when you finally get there all right let's get the boring Logistics out of the way first tip number one is get the Jordan pass getting the Jordan pass takes less than 3 minutes and if you're staying in Jordan for more than 3 nights it is a no-brainer purchasing this pass waves your tourist visa fees when you enter the country and it also includes prepaid entry to Petra as well as 40 other attractions but you must stay a minimum of three nights in Jordan in order to wave the tourist visa fees the Jordan pass with one day admission to Petra is 70 jod without the pass you would spend 90 jod on Visa entry fees and a one-day ticket to Petra this 20 din savings equates to about $28 per person tip number two is to reserve a rental car so Petra is about 2 and 1/2 hours from Queen ellia International Airport and it's a pretty easy Drive Google Maps don't let us down but having a rental car really allows you to explore Jordan and see all of the sites on your own terms also you can stop and buy snacks whenever you want which is a huge plus especially for me tip number three is to plan to stay two nights in wiim Musa Petra is a UNESCO world heritage site a wonder of the world and a filming location for iconic movies like Indiana Jones so it's no surprise that this attraction brings over 1 million visitors each year for that reason you're going to want to get there early the park opens at 6:00 a.m. and we definitely recommend that you take advantage of that after a long day of hiking you're not going to feel like driving so staying another night in wiim Musa lets you enjoy dinner and and catch Petra at night we used points to book two nights at the Petra Marriott Hotel the hotel is about a 10-minute drive from the park entrance and most of the restaurants in wiim Musa the hotel's super nice and the staff was really accommodating and friendly tip number four is to pack good hiking shoes so when we were packing for this trip we were debating whether or not we wanted to use precious real estate on our hiking shoes but we have walked in sand and on rock and loose gravel so all in all I would recommend packing the hiking shoes if you're also planning on visiting the run protected area while you're in Jordan you're not going to want to walk those sand dunes and normal tennis shoes tip number five is to go beyond the treasury I feel like when people talk about Petra think about Petra they really just think of like that one place the treasury but this place is huge while the treasury is the most iconic feature of Petra it can also be the most crowded so continue past the treasury to the ader trail to see the monastery and enjoy some tea with the view as a bonus tip a lot of you instagrammers have probably seen pictures that look a lot like this up here at the monastery is where you can find that awesome photo spot that little hole in the rock is the famous Instagram spot we also highly recommend the AL cabut Trail to see the treasury from above when you get here a lot of people will try to sell you a view of the treasury from above and we've read online that a lot of those views are un safe cuz they like take you up these like really quick routes we're hiking the long route it's basically a whole bunch of stairs up the back of the mountain climb all the way to the top and you go down the hill and there's just these guys that have like a Juice Shop you get coffee and tea and it's like a perfect panoram view of the treasury and it was really awesome and if you don't enjoy hiking don't worry because there will be plenty of opportunities to ride a camel or a donkey to the top which brings us to tip number six decide if you want to pay for a tour to a photo spot or for a camel ride and then be prepared to tell a lot of people no just to kind of set like realistic expectations there's a lot of vendors and people asking if you want to ride a camel or asking if you want to ride a donkey or trying to take you to a picture spot they're out there trying to make a living but you realize like I can't possibly say yes to all of you tip number seven is to remember to negotiate coming from the United States it's not really in our culture to negotiate prices Lauren was an easy target and and uh we just spent 30 JD on these two and they were definitely like $2 but it's always nice to help people out oh two dinar three dinar and we ended up paying like 30 however while and Jordan to get the best deal it is acceptable to negotiate price on goods and services and now our eighth and final tip see Petra by night and this will take some Advanced planning as Petra by night only runs every Monday Wednesday and Thursday of each week so it's really cool they have you can kind of see them behind us but they they light the walkway with candles and then you get to the treasury bunch of lights and a light show and music and it's supposed to be like a peaceful event everyone drinks tea and kind of hangs out there are no Advanced reservations for Pedra by night but there are unlimited tickets so you don't have to worry about it selling out tickets cost 17 Jordanian dinar or about 24 USD and can be purchased from your hotel or at the visitor center additionally make sure you hold on to your site ticket from the earlier in the day as you will need to present your valid daylight ticket with your show ticket at the entry gate to gain entrance to Petra by night if you want to follow along with the rest of our travels in Jordan or watch our full Petra Vlog we would love it if you would subscribe to our Channel okay

recommendations: Check out these chapters

videos.title: The DON'Ts of Visiting Jordan & Petra
videos.description:
Jordan is an incredible country for tourists to visit. With UNESCO World Heritage sights, fantastic food, and friendly people travelers have enjoyed visiting Jordan for years. Here are some tourists tips and information for visiting Petra and Jordan in general. 
Jordan tourist information.
Filmed in Jordan

➤ Book your accommodation and experiences:
Signia by Hilton: https://booking.tpk.lv/cUpIpo4k
Landmark Amman Hotel & Conference Center: https://booking.tpk.lv/LoDvuaih

Private or sharing: Jerash,Ajloun Castle & Umm Qais Day Tour: https://getyourguide.tpk.lv/W5VzapDb

videos.transcript: Hey there fellow travelers, Mark and Jocelyn. With Walter's World and today we're here in Petra in Jordan. And today we have for you are the don'ts of visiting Petra and some other don'ts of visiting Jordan. And I think Jocelyn should do the first don't of just visiting Jordan in general. One, don't expect toilet paper everywhere. The Jordanians use water. That's what that hose is by the toilet. So, you can choose to do that or you can choose to bring your own toilet paper. You might find it in some places, but just don't count on it. And also, you might need to pay a lady for it like when you come across the border. So, do be aware of that. Also, if you do use toilet paper, what do you not do? You do not flush your toilet paper here. You put it in the bin next to the toilet. you come to Petra, one thing I got to tell you is don't sleep in. You want to get here early so you can beat the really hot weather in the afternoon, but also beat a lot of the crowds cuz the tour groups are really coming in full strength like about noon, 10:00 noon. So, you want to get here a bit earlier. It does open up at 6:00 so you know that. Honestly, don't wait don't wait to go in the afternoon cuz the cruise ships come in. It's not like I got it's just a tour, but the cruise ships just come in wave after wave after wave. I saw buses 1 through 8 go through. I mean, they just come in loads about noon, 11:00 noon, something like that. They're really starting to pack it in. So, do get that morning one like I said, okay? It's it You're it'll make a difference. Another thing I would tell you is when you're coming here, don't expect to get a lot of free time in July and August. That is the peak time when people come here even though it's the hottest time to come here. It is the most popular. So, if you can come in the spring and fall, you really can reduce the crowds quite a bit so you're not kind of like rush through things or having people walk through your pictures all the time. So, something to kind of think about. I want to tell you now, don't be deceived by the maps of Petra. This place is huge. It's a giant complex. And yes, you can do it in a day, but don't think you should stay for two or three days to really explore everything when you're here especially if you get the Jordan Pass. You can get it where it's a couple days here, three days here, you know, and you can visit more and really see all the sites because honestly, you can't see it all in one day. It's just too long cuz doing it all, if you just do it quickly, it's like 7 or 8 hours of hiking and and it's really tough and really dry. So, you want to be safe with it and enjoy it. So, maybe do like one day, "Hey, we're just going to do Siq and we're going to do the you know, the Treasury and see that and then maybe go up to the you know, to the theater." And then the next day we're going to do the monastery. Kind of split it up because otherwise, you're going to get worn out and it can be unsafe. You know, cuz it just wears you out with the heat and the sun and all the hiking. So, do be aware of that cuz you should be safe and have a good time when you're here, but don't just do a day. Spend as much time as you can here. You got hotels are really nice when you're staying here. Yeah, don't don't think they don't have good hotels here. You can get some good hotels when you're staying here. So, that can be a nice experience as well. Also, if you're here in the evening on Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, they do have Petra at night where they basically candles lit up and it's kind of a cool thing. I mean, don't think like a huge light show or anything, but it is kind of a cool experience to have you are when you do have when you're here. Unfortunately, we're not here when it's day, so I can't show you any of our pictures from it cuz we don't have any cuz we weren't here for it, but that is one of the reasons why it might be good to stay a couple nights when you're here. So, you come to Petra, you don't have to have a guide to go through. You can There's plenty of books and things that can educate you on this. You can watch lots of videos for it, but honestly, don't pass up getting a guide, an official guide when you are here. Like you can actually get them when you come into the complex. There's actually guides you can hire there and or you can arrange your guide beforehand because honestly, the guides really bring out more of the history. Instead of just looking at this, they give you the history behind it. They tell you what's here. They explain the flash flooding that can happen here and they talk about the canals on the side. Well, I don't have to do that. The water canals, how they got water down here. They do a really good job of really making it come alive. So, don't pass up a guide. I mean, what I'd recommend if you're going to be here for a few days, do your first day have a guide take you to some of the spots like the first part, just so you have a good feeling of what this is all about and how it was made and then come and explore and hike the other days you're here, but yeah, don't don't pass up a guide. You don't have to have one, but don't think you don't don't have to have one either. You are going to take so many pictures and so many videos when you're here from this seek to the treasury to like just everything and just like you can't help yourself. You're going to take a lot. So, don't forget to bring a backup battery cuz you will burn through your phone a couple of times. So, bring a good backup charger just so you know that you can actually keep taking pictures and keep taking videos and you don't run out before you even get to the treasury. This is so cool. Like you will be so pumped. I mean honestly, don't don't crack your face when you have the big smile on your face when you come around the corner outside of the canyon here and you walk into the treasury cuz dude, it's so cool. Sadly though, don't expect to be able to go inside the treasury. It's really cool from the outside. You can see all this, but you don't get to go inside as a tourist. Another don't I have for you is don't forget there's actually two paths you can go up if you want to get that picture from above. If you're not very good with steps and uneven steps at that, maybe don't get it. I mean it's a cool shot, but if you don't think your knees can hold up or you're unsteady with the with your footing, I don't recommend doing it, but you can do that. There's two ones. One, you get a guy that will take you up about 20 30 minutes depending on how slow or fast you are. Then there's the long long one that can take a long time, but it's a free one. You don't need to do it with the guide. So, there are all those options if you want to get that above the treasury view, but even from down here you can't go wrong with those views, huh? Another thing you should realize, you can ride a horse when you are here, but don't think the horse takes you very far. You just come to this seek. You come to the entrance to the main canyon. That's all it does. So, do be aware of that and though they say it's free, you do have to tip the person that helps you get there and sometimes the horses go a little bit fast and for someone like myself, I could never do that to a horse where I would like totally smoosh them. And you see our friends here, you have a golf cart. So, if you have troubles walking, they do have a golf cart you can hire. Um you can have a driver take you and take you to the some of the spots. So, you have that if you do have limited mobility, so that can be a big that can be a game changer for a lot of people where they can't come here because honestly, don't bring your good shoes when you're walking around. I think I talked about that before, but this is one of those things, okay? Don't forget to watch out for the donkeys that might be running by. Um, also trotting camels cuz those are a thing. Um, but with all the sand, you don't really hear them until they're like right up on you. So, kind of keep an ear out and maybe check behind you now and then. So, when you come into Petra, you have your ticket and they'll scan it. But, one thing I got to tell you is don't forget to bring your passport when you come here cuz you do need it when you get scanned in. So, do be aware of that, okay? Don't forget your passport. Don't forget this. La Shukran. La Shukran means no, thank you, okay? Shukran is thank you and la is no, so la Shukran is no, thank you. You're going to need that because I don't want you to be pressured by the sales people here because they'll come up and again and again and again offer you a guide to take you on a hike, take you on a side quest, if you will, explain more stuff for you, have you do a horse ride, all kinds of stuff. Don't feel pressured by them, okay? They'll be there. You can just go on by and say, "La Shukran." And they'll be okay with it, all right? So, just know that that you might be dealing with some high-pressure sales tips and stuff and people coming up quite often, but it's okay. La Shukran will go a long way to show that you learned a couple words and to show respect, but also that, "Hey, thank you, but no, thank you right now." And one of the things I got to say is don't believe all the promises those people do promise you, whether it's a guided tour or a free trip or a free walk or a free horse ride or whatever cuz tipping will be involved and I've talked about that or I will talk about that. So, just have a heads-up. Don't be surprised if you get tired walking in Petra. One thing I got to say is don't think that Petra is just the Treasury. There's a lot of other places you can go to. There's the You got the theater over here. You got the monastery, which is really cool, though that's like about a 90-minute keep on hiking from the Treasury, but there's a lot of other things to go do, like hike up some of the side trails and go through there, check out some of the caves. I mean, I know you can't go in the Treasury, but there's so many other things you can do when you're here that it isn't just seeing the Treasury and going, "Wow, Indiana Jones." and walking back. No, you're going to explore more. I mean, seriously, the Treasury is cool and all, but the tombs? Dude, this is so cool. You can't go wrong when you're here in Petra. Now, I know I talked about the aggressive sellers and people trying to off you service and stuff, but don't pass up the conversations with the sellers cuz some of them are pretty funny. The guy was offering, "Tea, coffee? Come have a Kit Kat. You look like you need a break." I mean, the people are pretty funny with it, whether they're little kids bringing you the shops or adults, you can have some fun conversations with them. And they would play along with the tourists, so that can be kind of a funny thing, but yeah, it is kind of a cool thing. And if you talk to them for a while, why not pick up a souvenir from when you're here, okay? I think another important don't to have when you come here to Jordan is don't think Jordan is just Petra. I mean, Petra's UNESCO World Heritage, it's gorgeous. You want to come here, that's number one thing to do, but going to Wadi Rum, the desert where you have the sandstone kind of like shaves in there when you're hiking around, I mean, it's just incredible. If you want to see Roman ruins, you go up to Jerash. And we were up there and it was fantastic just to see the incredible size city that these there that you can see. Going to Amman or maybe you could just go down to the coast with all the Jordanians who enjoy the coast in Aqaba. I mean, there are other things to do when you are here than Petra, so don't forget about those, too. Also, specifically looking at Wadi Rum, I would tell you this, don't just show up without a tour. When you go there, you're going to want to take a tour to do the hikes and go explore through the desert, but you needed a tour with it. And if you come in a busy season, there might not be anything available for you. So, to give yourself that peace of mind, don't forget to book your tour of Wadi Rum before you go, so it's all set up, okay? One thing to realize when you're coming to Jordan, they have this thing called the Jordan Pass, which it can be really helpful as a traveler, cuz it gets you to like 40 sites, gives you a couple of days at Petra, pays for your visa to come in, so that's kind of nice. But, my don't for that is don't get the Jordan Pass if you're only going to come for Petra. You're only coming for one night from Israel over to Jordan going back. It's not really worth it that way. But, that's just one of those things that the Jordan Pass is super helpful if you're going to explore Jordan. Don't pass up getting it. There's two main roads to go from the north to the south that you're probably going to be using whether you're going from Petra to Amman, but the driving here is actually pretty simple to do, pretty easy. Now, some of the driving don'ts when you are here in Jordan, um don't drive at night because with the animals going across the road and how people come on and sometimes lack of lights on some people's cars, it can be a bit dangerous. So, let's try to stay driving during the day. Also, don't forget if people are passing you, move over a little bit because it feels like the shoulder is another lane you're going to use when you're here in Jordan. I've got a hat and sunblock in my bag, got stuff for the kids. You do want to have that just so you can enjoy yourself and not worried about turning into a purple tourist and, you know, a farmer tan version. And when you're going around and you're seeing the markets or you're going to buy souvenirs, don't forget um you can negotiate here and you should negotiate here, especially at major tourist places, whether you're in Petra or here in Jerash or other places. If they're trying to sell you stuff and there's no price tag on there, just know that first price, shoot for half of what they offer at first to give your give yourself a good idea of what you probably should be paying when it comes to negotiation side of thing. Going along with the sun protection, another don't I have for you is don't forget to bring something warm. I know this sounds silly cuz you're thinking, "Jordan, isn't it like deserty and hot there all the time?" Well, no, it's not. If you're in the wintertime, you know, there's places it does snow here in Jordan, but also, if you're going to be on the desert and things, it gets chilly at night and even in the summertime, you might want to throw on a jacket. I know our kids were down in Petra, they're like, "Uh Dad, can we go back and get our sweatshirts?" So, I mean, these are things you need to think about if you're going to be out, especially if you're going to do like the if you go to Petra and you go to Petra at night, I would definitely bring some a cover-up for that just so you have something to keep yourself a little bit warm because though it feels hot and sunny all day long, that heat doesn't stay with you in the evening time, or at least not every evening time. Now, another thing that's important to remember when you are here in Jordan is you don't forget to tip. If you go out to a restaurant, it's going to be 10% is fine for a tip. You can, of course, go more than that, but you do want to tip, but also, you're going to tip your guides when you go around and then I know some people feel, "Well, I paid for the guide service. Why do I need to tip them?" Well, you do tip them. Tip them about 10% of your tour. That's usually kind of a nice thing to do, but of course, tipping is up to you if you want to do it or not, but it's just one of those things you have here and when you pay with a credit card, what you'll do is just tell them like the final price or you sometimes they'll say, "Hey, how much do you want to tip?" You just tell them. It's not like you hide it like in America where you write it down later so they can never see what you actually tip or if you did tip, but it is something to kind of think about when you are paying. So, what you'll get is just a final credit card receipt that has the total like check you paid and tip included, so you know exactly what to expect to come out of your your account. So, you do have that, all right? So, don't forget to tip your waiters and your guides when you are here. Now, if you're going to be going to the Dead Sea when you are here in Jordan, you can go there. It's not a big deal. I do have some don'ts when you go there. One, don't shave or wax before you go to the Dead Sea. Take my word for it. It will burn, burn, burn. Also, do not dunk your head. Don't dive. Don't splash at people when you are there cuz the salt content in the water of the Dead Sea is so high, it burns your eyes. It burns those cuts. You get it in your mouth, you swallow it, you're probably going to throw up and get a little sick. So, don't do those things as well. Also, um don't wear your favorite swimsuit or wear any jewelry when you're going in cuz the high salt content will basically ruin your jewelry and probably might ruin your swimsuit as well. So, pack an extra one when you are there. Now, when you're here in Jordan, don't forget to eat Jordanian food. This right here, this is mansaf. And this is a very, very typical dish. You have this on Fridays. And what you do is you put a little of the yogurt in. Okay? You take the yogurt, you pour it in where you're going to be be eating. And then, this is very hot. So, come in close so you can see and you kind of mix the yogurt in with the rice. Okay? Mix that in there. Let it cool off a bit because don't be surprised your hands are dirty. That's why don't forget to wash your hands before you eat. Hands get messy, not dirty. Hands get messy. And you make a ball. Okay? If it's too hot, yes, you can put it on the plate. I'm not from here, so it's a little bit difficult, but you make your ball. But also, you take a little bit of the meat. I heard this is lamb. And so, it's lamb, rice, and this yogurt. And you make a ball. Little bit more. Obviously, my friend can do it better than I can, but uh you make this ball and then eat up. Now, I'm totally messing up. It's totally messy. And yes, you can use fork and knife and spoons. It's okay, but this is something you really want to have. Also, if you're looking for other Jordanian food, have you ever watched on Instagram when they flip over the pot with the rice and meat? Yeah, that's one thing. Shawarma, falafel, hummus, a lot of great food right here. But, we got to eat. Now, as a hungry traveler, I think there's some don'ts I need to tell you about food here in Jordan. One, don't expect lunch to be the time you're used to, like noon. They eat at different times here. Breakfast between 9:00 and 10:00. That's kind of a breakfast time here. Lunch is at 3:00 p.m. And that's the big meal of the day. And then dinner is like 8:00 or 9:00 at night. It's kind of a smaller smaller affair. So, be aware of that. Also, don't be too concerned about your Jordanian friends um love affair with olive oil. They really love olive oil. Like really love olive oil. Like you'll see sometimes a meal might be bread with a bowl of olive oil and then some of the za'atar, which is wild thyme. And they'll dip the bread into the oil, put some of the za'atar on there, and that's dinner, okay? But, don't be upset. Don't think they're unhealthy. so do it first thing when you get there very early then you'll pretty much have it all for yourself like i had it second thing you don't want to miss in jordan is the dead sea now the dead sea is the lowest point of dry land on earth at 431 meters below sea level making for a fascinating natural wonder that no visitor to jordan should miss now it's known for its exceptionally salty water so i don't recommend you to drinking it or putting it in your eye now you can still float on your back while you put a macbeth on you just as i said just avoid getting it in your eyes the third thing you martian it was actually filmed there so you can't pretend you're walking around on mars when you're in jordan you don't want to miss the red sea now jordan might just have 26 kilometers off coastline as well as a beautiful view you don't want to miss when in jordan you don't want to miss going to jirach now jordan hosts the largest and most compelling roman site the ancient city of jiraj which is with columns on both sides paved with original stones so it's really really cool especially if you're a history nerd like like i am you don't want to miss vadi mucha i hope i pronounced that correctly videos.title: SMARTEST way to see PETRA / TIPS & HACKS/ JORDAN episode 1
videos.description:
This video is a Petra Jordan guide showing you the SMARTEST WAY TO SEE PETRA in JORDAN. This is Petra: episode 1 of my JORDAN travel series. You will see Petra in Jordan with a local Bedouin who was born and raised in the village and knows all the secrets, tips and hacks to get Petra with no tourists as well as all the best photo spots just for you. We will see the secret hike, backdoor entrance to the Petra Monastery with no oner else around, the Petra market with local life, Roman street, caves and gorges, the Siq trail and the famous treasury along with the treasury viewpoint. This Jordan Travel Guide video will demonstrate how to do a Petra day trip without getting up at 4am and still get famous spots, viewpoints and monuments to yourself. My Bedouin guide will also share surprises , hidden spots and fun facts that you would otherwise miss in Petra, Jordan. If you're looking for what to do in Jordan, this Jordan travel guide is perfect option for a day trip. One day in Petra is a fantastic way to see this wonder of the world and I hope you will find this video enjoyable and useful. Watch episode 1 of my Jordan series in 4K for best quality. 

Episode 2 from Wadi Rum: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=C03S3XUW0HA&t=3s
Episode 3 from Aqaba Red Sea: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lbHsHvAMjgU&t=3s
Episode 4 from the Dead Sea: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pJ8DP4lJMcA

Subscribe for more epic travel content: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCgQkZeS9_natdBe_xYwDrUQ

Notes: I paid in full for Abdullah's tour and this is not a collaboration. I sincerely recommend him as a Petra guide, he is fantastic and you can contact him here: +962777874294 .
The local currency is the JOD and you'll need around 20-30 JODs if you'd like to buy drinks in Petra during the day as well as access the transport to the backdoor hike. The entrance into Petra itself is included in your Jordan Pass which I strongly recommend getting online before your arrival into the country. 
I visited in mid September and got to Petra at around midday, my tour lasted just over 5 hours (I chose not to stop for lunch) and we walked 11km!
I was worried about dressing conservatively and respecting local custom but Petra is full of foreigners and I found no one blinks an eye if your shoulders/ knees aren't fully covered. 

my instagram: @kristinahouwer
my website: https://kristinahouwer.com/
my facebook: https://www.facebook.com/kristinahouwer
Copyright free music: https://share.epidemicsound.com/6p818n


TIME STAMPS:

0:00 INTRO
1:08 Petra secret hike / backdoor entrance
1:50 Local life
03:08 Petra Monastery
4:23 Petra market
5:09 This might shock you 
5:45 Hidden spots, surprises, fun facts in Petra
6:49 Roman street
7:22 Petra Treasury
7:41 Petra Treasury viewpoint
8:36 Petra Jordan Siq Trail
8:52 OUTTRO
9:23 In the Next Episode...

recommendations: Watch episode 1 of my Jordan series in 4K for best quality
I sincerely recommend him as a Petra guide, he is fantastic and you can contact him here: +962777874294
The entrance into Petra itself is included in your Jordan Pass which I strongly recommend getting online before your arrival into the country
I visited in mid September and got to Petra at around midday, my tour lasted just over 5 hours (I chose not to stop for lunch) and we walked 11km
